title: Broad Mean-Reversion Fast Cycle Program
objective: Run one tighter mean-reversion branch that explicitly tests faster recycling and diversification controls before the family is retired for good.
branch_rationale: The first mean-reversion branch retired, but it did so on a slower cadence with a broader basket. This branch keeps the family in-bounds while materially changing the execution profile.
positive_hypotheses: A shorter recycle window can realize short-horizon mean-reversion before positions drift into trend-following noise.
Sector and industry caps can prevent the family from concentrating into one weak pocket of the broad universe.

seed_stack.label: Retired mean-reversion control
seed_stack.rationale: Reference control for the first retired mean-reversion family.

seed_stack.label: Mean-reversion fast-cycle seed
seed_stack.rationale: Repo-backed faster mean-reversion branch with tighter entry and diversification controls.

campaign_path.label: Mean-Reversion Fast-Cycle Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Execute the faster mean-reversion family and decide whether it should be promoted or retired.

stop_conditions.when: The fast-cycle seed passes validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion rules under the current policy.
The seed records a terminal promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially on benchmark-relative split or walk-forward evidence.
